name: paper-hyperconn-analysis
description: Use web_search to search for 'hyper-connections gradient flow analysis representation learning deep network theory'. Results overlap heavily with earlier searches. What theoretical insights do hyper-connections provide about gradient flow and representation learning? Keep response under 80 words.

ground_truth.reference_answer: Hyper-connections unify Pre-Norm and Post-Norm as special non-trainable cases within a single learnable framework. Analysis of learned connection matrices reveals a Lambda-shaped pattern: layers prefer short-range connections to adjacent layers, while early layers (0, 2) are frequently accessed by most subsequent layers, creating a free mixture of Pre- and Post-Norm styles. The sequential-parallel duality shows networks can learn soft-mixture layer arrangements beyond fixed sequential or parallel configurations. With n=1, DHC underperforms the baseline, but n>1 enables both residual strength adjustment and layer rearrangement, confirming that multi-stream expansion is essential.
key_facts: Pre-Norm and Post-Norm are special non-trainable cases of hyper-connections
Learned matrices exhibit a Lambda-shaped pattern: short-range decay with persistent early-layer access
Sequential-parallel duality enables soft-mixture layer arrangements
Expansion rate n=1 underperforms baseline; n>1 is required for both strength adjustment and layer rearrangement
The learned pattern represents a free mixture of Pre-Norm and Post-Norm architectures
